data.title: Pharvaris GAAP EPS of -€0.85
data.description:
• Pharvaris press release (NASDAQ:PHVS): Q3 GAAP EPS of -€0.85.
  • Liquidity Position. Cash and cash equivalents were €236 million as of March 31, 2025, compared to €281 million for December 31, 2024.

data.title: DiaMedica targets preeclampsia Phase 2 dose selection and ReMEDy2 interim analysis in H1 2026 as enrollment momentum builds
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: DiaMedica Therapeutics Inc. (DMAC) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Rick Pauls, President and CEO, stated that "we continue to make substantial progress in both of our clinical development programs." He provided an update on the preeclampsia program, noting that Part 1a of the Phase 2 investigator-sponsored trial is nearing target dose identification for advancement to Part 1b. Pauls indicated that preliminary top-line results are expected to be released between the second half of June and the first half of July, depending on laboratory schedules.
  • Pauls highlighted that May is Preeclampsia Awareness Month and the company will sponsor a key opinion leader call on May 28. He emphasized that "DM199 is the only novel agent currently being studied in pregnant women with preeclampsia."
  • For the stroke program, Pauls reported that participant enrollment is now between the 20th and 25th percentile mark for interim analysis and reiterated guidance for the interim analysis on the first 200 participants to be completed in the first half of 2026. He also noted the engagement of an experienced stroke neurologist to support site engagement during Dr. Lorianne Masuoka’s short-term medical leave.
  • Scott Kellen, Chief Financial Officer, reported, "As of March 31, 2025, we reported a total combined cash and investments of 37.3 million, current liabilities of 4.7 million, and working capital of 32.8 million." Kellen stated, "We anticipate that our current cash and investments provide us a runway into Q3 of 2026." He added, "Our research and development expenses increased to 5.7 million for the 3 months ended March 31, 2025, up from 3.7 million for the 3 months ended March 31, 2024."

Outlook

  • Management expects to release preliminary top-line results from the Part 1a proof-of-concept portion of the preeclampsia trial between the second half of June and first half of July 2025. Guidance was reiterated that the interim analysis for the ReMEDy2 stroke program will be completed in the first half of 2026. The company anticipates R&D expenses to moderately increase as ReMEDy2 expands globally and the DM199 preeclampsia program continues.

Financial Results

  • CFO Kellen reported a decrease in combined cash and investments and working capital from the end of December 2024, due primarily to net cash used to fund operations. Net cash used in operating activities for Q1 2025 was 7.1 million compared to 6.7 million for Q1 2024. R&D expenses increased due to the continuation of the ReMEDy2 trial, global expansion, increased manufacturing, and clinical team expansion. General and administrative expenses were 2.5 million for Q1 2025, primarily reflecting noncash share-based compensation from a board member’s retirement. Net other income was 443,000 for Q1 2025, down from 597,000 in Q1 2024, due to lower interest income.

Q&A

  • Thomas Flaten, Lake Street: Asked if the variable for the Part 1a readout timing was primarily the assay for DM199 crossing the placental barrier. Pauls confirmed, "that's the main item is going to be the placental transfer" and timing depends on laboratory processing.
  • Flaten followed up on triggers for Parts 2 and 3 of the preeclampsia study. Pauls stated that dilation of intrauterine arteries would prompt movement to the fetal growth restriction cohort and that more details would follow with upcoming results.
  • Matthew Caufield, H.C. Wainwright: Asked about read-through or de-risking between preeclampsia and AIS development. Pauls noted the indications are unique but said "a positive effect here in preeclampsia...will this be another confirmation that this protein is active," referencing existing use of similar proteins in Asia.
  • Chase Knickerbocker, Craig-Hallum: Asked for details on stroke enrollment and high-volume centers. Pauls explained some high enrolling sites are seeing "1 to 2 patients per site per month" and that overall, "we are above our plan here now, and we're encouraged with the momentum."
  • Knickerbocker asked about the total number of centers and international enrollment. Pauls replied, "we're currently in mid-30s" for centers, including international sites in Georgia.
  • Flaten returned to ask if Parts 2 and 3 of the preeclampsia study would be based in South Africa or expand to the U.S. Pauls said they remain under the same protocol and collaborators "will not need to go back for regulatory clearance" but future expansion to the U.S. and globally is planned.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral to slightly positive, with questions focused on operational execution and timing details rather than fundamental challenges.
  • Management maintained a confident and informative tone, particularly when discussing enrollment momentum and trial progress. Pauls used phrases such as "we are above our plan" and "we're encouraged with the momentum" to signal confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment remained steady, with continued interest in operational progress. Management’s tone was consistent, though this quarter included more explicit statements about being encouraged by enrollment trends.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for the ReMEDy2 interim analysis timeline remained unchanged (“first half of 2026”).
  • The preeclampsia program advanced, with this quarter’s focus on imminent top-line results and dose selection for Part 1b, compared to last quarter’s emphasis on initial dosing and safety.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on enrollment rates, protocol expansion, and study logistics, but this quarter saw more questions on operational momentum and triggers for study advancement.
  • Management confidence and sentiment remained strong, with both quarters highlighting steady progress and optimism.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged that the timing of preliminary preeclampsia results depends on outside laboratory processing schedules.
  • CFO Kellen noted increased net cash used in operating activities and rising R&D expenses, primarily from trial expansion.
  • There was mention of Dr. Masuoka being on short-term medical leave; an experienced stroke neurologist has been engaged to support site engagement and maintain momentum.
  • No new regulatory or operational risk factors were introduced, and management reiterated confidence in trial progress and cash runway.

Final Takeaway

DiaMedica’s management emphasized substantial progress in both the preeclampsia and stroke clinical programs, with preeclampsia Phase 2 dose selection and top-line results expected soon and ReMEDy2 stroke trial enrollment showing encouraging momentum. The company reaffirmed its cash runway into Q3 2026 and maintained guidance for key milestones, underscoring confidence in both programs’ advancement and continued operational execution.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SA analysts weigh in on the S&P 500’s latest moves
data.description:
Seeking Alpha analysts weigh in on the status of the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) as some market participants believe the recent uptrend on Wall Street will continue whereas others say this is just a bear market rally as they are waiting for the next shoe to drop.

The opinions of SA analysts arrive as the S&P 500 on Tuesday turned positive for the 2025 trading year for the first time since the end of February, with the index experiencing a volatile ride since “Liberation Day.” 

See what SA analysts have to say below:

SA analyst Oakoff Investments stated: “The S&P 500 surged over 12.5% due to trade war de-escalation and strong Q1 earnings, but the rally's foundation appears fragile and unsustainable,” in SPY: The Correction May Come Soon. 

SA analyst John Bowman outlined: “Ultimately, this CPI report was very good and should unite optimists about the economy… For now, I am bullish now seeing that businesses have been able to mitigate the challenges of volatile trade policy so far, given that we are about a month into following the big announcement,” in Another Good CPI Reading Is Exactly What We Needed. 

SA analyst Damir Tokic said: “The S&P 500 Index (SP500) spiked by around 3% and above the key resistance of 200dma on the news about the 90-day delay in reciprocal tariffs between the U.S. and China. Although the technical picture looks better, as long as the price stays above 200dma, the fundamentals still point to the bear market, and thus this is still a likely a bear market rally,” in Peak Trade War De-Escalation, Sell The Rip. 

SA analyst Lance Roberts spotlighted: “The market’s technical backdrop remains a near-term concern. While momentum and relative strength have improved, those factors are beginning to wane, suggesting buyers may be reaching near-term exhaustion,” in A Bear Market Rally? Or, Just A Correction? 

For investors looking to track the overall movement of the S&P 500 (SP500), several index ETFs and mutual funds are worth considering.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

  • PMEC shares fall 4% on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Fed needs to be 'steady hand' in uncertain times, Chicago Fed's Goolsbee says
data.description:
The Federal Reserve's "job is to be the steady hand, not respond to the daily gyrations either of the stock market or of policy pronouncements," Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee said in a media interview aired on Wednesday.

So far, hard economic is showing a solid economy, he said. For example, Tuesday's Consumer Price Index was better than many had feared and didn't show a jump in inflation. As such, he implied the tariff drama in April hasn't had time to filter into the data.

"When there are moments of a lot of dust in the air — like what we saw a bit in April — you can go into some of this paralysis, but it still takes some time for that to show up in the numbers," he said in an interview on NPR.

Overall, he thinks the U.S. economy is still in decent shape. "Now, we got a bunch of noise that we're trying to figure out the through-line. But if we can past that, I feel like underneath there, that the solid hard-data economy is still there."

"We're still kind of holding our breath and if consumers or businesses start freaking out a little bit about uncertainty and pull back on their spending or pull back on their investment, you can get some downturns," he added.

He emphasized the importance of not placing too much weight on a month or two of data. "It's just not realistic to expect that businesses or central banks to be jumping to conclusions about long-term things when you've got so much short-term variability. That's just a very difficult environment," Goolsbee said.

Gathering more information directly from people and businesses can also provide more information on which way the economy is headed before it hits the numbers.

"The official data only come out with a lag," he said. "That's why it's, at moments of transition, extra important to get out and listen to the people in real time what they're seeing."

data.title: Meritage Homes announces COO exit
data.description:
Homebuilder Meritage Homes (NYSE:MTH) said in a filing on Tuesday that COO Clint Szubinski will leave the company for personal reasons.

Szubinski's anticipated final date of employment will be May 16.

data.title: VictoryArc's Talia pitches Tel Aviv Stock Exchange as long at Sohn Conf
data.description:
• Joseph Talia, founder and chief investment officer at VictoryArc Holdings, recommends buying shares of Tel Aviv Stock Exchange (OTCPK:TVAVF).
  • He presented his idea on Wednesday at the Sohn Conference in New York. He believes the stock could triple in the next five years. He also believes the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange may be a potential takeover candidate.

--reported by Rob Williams in New York

data.title: Curtiss-Wright announces additional $400M share repurchase program
data.description:
Curtiss-Wright (NYSE:CW) announced on Wednesday the authorization of an additional $400M share repurchase program, bringing the total available authorization to $534M.

The company said it expects to minimally repurchase $66M worth of shares in 2025.

Additionally, the company declared $0.24/share quarterly dividend, 14.3% increase from prior dividend of $0.21.

data.title: Tempus partners with Boehringer Ingelheim to develop cancer treatments
data.description:
• Tempus AI (NASDAQ:TEM) has announced a new multi-year collaboration with Boehringer Ingelheim to develop Boehringer’s expanding oncology pipeline.
  • As part of the agreement, Boehringer will gain access to Tempus’ de-identified database, which includes integrated molecular, clinical, and imaging data, along with Lens, Tempus’ proprietary AI-driven analytical platform.

data.title: Gladstone Investment signals cautious optimism for new buyouts while maintaining $0.96 per share annual dividend
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Gladstone Investment (GAIN) Q4 2025

Management View

  • David Gladstone, Chairman and CEO, opened the call by reaffirming commitment to delivering updates and views on the business environment, noting "our two goals for this call is to help you understand what happened and also give you some current views on what I think the future will be like."
  • President David Dullum highlighted that "GAIN did produce very positive results for the fourth quarter and the fiscal year ended March 31, 2025," reporting adjusted NII of $0.97 per share, which covers the $0.96 per share annual dividend. Dullum noted the portfolio's total fair value at $979 million as of March 31, 2025, with new buyouts and a significant realized capital gain of $19.8 million from a portfolio exit. He stated, "for the year we invested a total of $221 million, which is up from $184 million in the prior year."
  • Dullum mentioned continued monthly distribution of $0.08 per share and supplemental distributions aggregating to $1.66 per share for the year. Additional $0.54 per share supplemental distribution was declared in April.
  • The company added experienced talent to the investing team to support portfolio growth, now managing 25 operating companies.
  • Dullum discussed ongoing cautious optimism about new buyout activity and the need to assess tariff impacts on costs, supply chain, and customer demand: "We are very far along and expect to close two new acquisitions shortly, if not by the end of this quarter."
  • CFO Taylor Ritchie stated, "we had a strong finish to the fiscal year, generating total investment income of $93.7 million, up from $87.3 million in the prior year." Ritchie added that adjusted net investment income for the year was $35.5 million or $0.97 per share.

Outlook

  • Dullum indicated, "from our perspective, there continues to be good liquidity in the M&A market. It is a very competitive environment, though we have now added variables of course regarding tariffs." He reported being "cautiously optimistic for our new buyout activity during the year" and expects to close two new acquisitions soon.
  • The company remains cautious regarding consumer-focused companies affected by tariffs, with management working on supply chain alternatives and production strategies.
  • Ritchie stated, "we ended the fiscal year with $55.3 million or $1.50 per share in spillover, sufficient to cover our current monthly distribution of $0.08 per share for an annual run rate of $0.96 per share and the recently declared $0.54 per share supplemental distribution to be paid in June."

Financial Results

  • Ritchie reported total investment income for the fourth quarter at $27.5 million, up from $21.4 million in the prior quarter, driven by increases in dividend and success fee income and interest income.
  • Net expenses for the quarter were $20.3 million, slightly higher than $20.2 million in the previous quarter.
  • Net investment income for the quarter was $7.2 million compared to $1.2 million last quarter. Adjusted net investment income was $9.4 million or $0.26 per share, up from $8.6 million or $0.23 per share.
  • Aggregate portfolio valuations, excluding a $24.3 million reversal of unrealized appreciation, were up $14.1 million.
  • Net asset value (NAV) increased to $13.55 per share from $13.30 per share at the end of the prior quarter.
  • The company issued $126.5 million of publicly traded notes, upsized its credit facility to $270 million, and had $214 million in credit line availability as of the release date.

Q&A

  • Mickey Schleien, Ladenburg: Asked for quantitative sense of tariff risk exposure. David Dullum responded, "the correct answer is probably most of them in some regards... with the exception of one or two, none that we are, I would say, overly concerned about," adding that companies directly affected have been shifting production and building up inventory ahead of tariffs.
  • Schleien: Queried exposure to lower income customers in the consumer sector. Dullum said, "I don't think we have any that I would really consider... a much lower income type category."
  • Schleien: Asked for updates on non-accruals, especially Hobbs. Dullum answered, "we still have four companies on non-accrual and actually three of them are all profitable, including Hobbs... I'm hopeful we might bringing them back on accrual by the end of this year."
  • Schleien: Inquired about the situation with Horizon. Dullum replied, "Horizon is very profitable actually, and its EBITDA, again, has continued to improve."
  • Erik Zwick, Lucid Capital Markets: Asked about the rationale for dividend recap at Educators Resource and potential for more. Dullum explained, dividend recaps are considered when there is confidence in management and business fundamentals, stating, "it's usually around a couple of things. One, we have a management team that has got investment in the company and it's an opportunity for them to get some liquidity."
  • Zwick: Requested spillover amount. Ritchie confirmed, "we ended the year with $55.3 million of spillover, which is at the current level, $1.50 per share."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' questions were neutral to slightly cautious, focusing on risk exposure to tariffs, non-accrual portfolio companies, and the sustainability of distributions.
  • Management's tone was positive and confident in prepared remarks, with Dullum stating, "our current portfolio, we feel is in good shape." During Q&A, management maintained a measured but optimistic stance, especially in addressing non-accruals and tariff concerns.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from confident growth and buyout activity to more cautious optimism due to tariff concerns, while analysts maintained a similar, measured approach.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw adjusted NII at $0.26 per share, up from $0.23 per share in the third quarter.
  • Portfolio fair value decreased to $979 million from $1.1 billion the previous quarter, attributed to asset exits and realized gains.
  • Buyout investment activity remained strong, with $221 million deployed for the year, up from $184 million previously.
  • Management highlighted a more cautious outlook regarding tariffs' impact and consumer demand, compared to a more growth-focused view last quarter.
  • Analysts' focus remained on underlying risks and sustainability of income, while management emphasized operational resilience and active risk management.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified tariffs as a significant variable, requiring careful risk assessment and forecasting of impacts on costs, demand, and supply chain dynamics.
  • The company is monitoring consumer-focused companies for margin and demand impacts due to tariff-driven price increases.
  • Four portfolio companies remain on non-accrual status, though management is optimistic about improvement, particularly at Hobbs.
  • Dullum noted, "we are working with all of our companies in evaluating supply chain alternatives and various production strategies so that we can navigate the current environment."

Final Takeaway

Gladstone Investment delivered a strong fourth quarter and fiscal year, fully covering its regular dividend and declaring a new supplemental distribution. The company remains cautiously optimistic on new buyout activity, with two acquisitions expected to close soon, while actively managing tariff-related risks and maintaining a strong liquidity position. Management expressed confidence in the current portfolio's health and reiterated a commitment to ongoing distributions and disciplined investment strategy amid an evolving economic landscape.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
